summary: Phase-2 deep review of the 5.3.7 spec bundle (post-panel-fix working tree): tasks/nebula-frontend.md + coding-your-ui.md + api-reference.md + resources.md

dropped.finding: Teardown has two documented entry points — factory-returned `dispose()` and `client.dispose()` ('Also available on the factory return') — with no statement of which is canonical, and the client method tearing down factory-owned state (debounce timers, effectScopes) is layering-odd. Two right ways to do the same thing is the exact walled-garden smell the docs otherwise avoid.
dropped.why: False positive: the alias relationship IS stated in exactly one clause (api-reference.md:366 "Also available on the factory return as `dispose()`"), and the doc structure plus coding-your-ui.md:37/:87 (which list `dispose` among client methods and pin flush-on-lifecycle to `client.dispose()`) consistently make `client.dispose()` canonical — the finding's preferred fix would break those cross-references; the "layering-odd" objection misreads the substrate, since NebulaClient moves into packages/nebula-frontend in v3 (nebula-frontend.md:43) and is already the store-integrated component (nebula-client.ts:425-429), so what's left is a taste-level relitigation of a coherent, cross-doc-consistent design.

result.synthesis:
# Synthesized Design-Review Findings — Ranked & Deduplicated

74 raw findings → 37 unique items after cross-lens dedup. Lenses: ARCH, SEC, TEST, PROD, CONS.

---

## BLOCKERS (4)

**B1. `addEdge` checks `write` on new parent only — self-service privilege escalation; flagship sharing flow throws as written** *(ARCH)*
- Location: `apps/nebula/src/dag-tree.ts:198-218`; `website/docs/nebula/api-reference.md:397-399`; `website/docs/nebula/resources.md:157-177`
- Any user with `write` on their own node can graft ANY tree node under their subtree and gain cascaded admin (substrate-shipped, reachable today). Simultaneously, resources.md Approach 2 (Alice adds `user-bob` as parent) requires `write` on user-bob, which Alice doesn't hold — the documented co-ownership flow throws PermissionDeniedError.
- Fix: require `write` on new parent AND `admin`/`write` on the child; rewrite Approach 2 as an explicit two-party flow; update dag-tree.ts, api-reference § client.dagTree, and resources.md before pinning the v3 surface.

**B2. ('lmz','dag') check-skip keyed inconsistently — type/prefix-keyed reading is a cross-user read/subscribe bypass** *(SEC; interacts with M2, M9)*
- Location: `tasks/nebula-frontend.md:758, :1056` vs `api-reference.md:439`; `subscriptions.ts`; `resources.ts:94`; `star.ts:531`
- Three statements of the skip disagree (exact tuple vs `lmz` type vs `lmz.*` prefix). Server addresses by resourceId alone; the type-mismatch check is the only rt→resource binding. A type-keyed skip lets `subscribe('lmz', '<any-rid>')` skip BOTH the type check and the permission check, delivering any victim resource's snapshot + live fanout (victim rids = guessable subs visible in the universal dag broadcast).
- Fix: pin the skip to the exact tuple (`rt==='lmz' && rid==='dag'`), routing exclusively to the synthesized snapshot (never Resources.read/Snapshots); any other rid under 'lmz' errors before storage. Align all three artifacts; add test: `subscribe('lmz', <app rid>)` → error, no Subscribers row.

**B3. `newETag` replay idempotency broken for creates — the overload-design ship-blocker is unmet and the test can't fail** *(TEST)*
- Location: `apps/nebula/src/resources.ts:374-393` vs `:437-450`; `test/.../nebula-client-read-transaction.test.ts:193-227`; `nebula-frontend.md` 5.3.8 ~L946; `resources.md` ~L237
- Step 7 throws "already exists" BEFORE Step 9a's idempotency short-circuit, so a genuine retry of any create-containing batch (first-run bootstrap, flagship addTodo) reports failure (`infrastructure-error`/timeout) and rolls back optimistic state for a write that landed. The only idempotency test swaps create→put on retry, so it cannot catch this; partial-churn multi-resource replay is enumerated nowhere — yet docs publish "retry-safe" as contract.
- Fix: v3 checklist item to hoist idempotency above op-existence validation; replace the 5.3.8 probe with an identical-ops replay matrix ({create, put, delete, create+put} × {no churn, third-party churn}); fix the wrong-order test comment.

**B4. `textMerge` examples pass server as `base` — 3-way merge degenerates to local-wins; true merge is unimplementable because `conflict-pending` carries no base** *(CONS + PROD converged)*
- Location: `resources.md:309, :486-499`; `api-reference.md:523-535 (§ textMerge), :600-605`; `nebula-frontend.md:371-376, :697, :866`
- `textMerge(server, local, base=server)` returns exactly `local` — the concurrent server edit is silently dropped, and the auto-registered `@longform` handler inherits the defect. Root cause: the `'conflict-pending'` payload exposes only local + server, no common ancestor — though the framework already captures pre-write snapshots (the rollback baseline).
- Fix: add `base: { value, eTag }` to the `'conflict-pending'` branch (sourced from the pre-write snapshot) before v3 locks the handler machinery; fix both examples and the @longform auto-registration spec.

---

## MAJORS (15)

**M1. First-run bootstrap "find my node" scan is non-deterministic, crashes for admins, and is exploitable** *(ARCH + SEC + PROD + CONS — 4 lenses converged)*
- Location: `coding-your-ui.md:270-287`; `dag-tree.ts:157-158`; `dag-ops.ts:136-171`; `resources.md:125-153`
- `[...dag.permissions].find(([, grants]) => grants.has(sub))![0]` fails three ways: (1) Star admins authorize via `claims.access.admin` bypass and may hold zero grants → `!` throws TypeError in top-level await → blank app for the demo's first user; (2) any-tier/any-node match in ascending-nodeId order means Approach-1 sharees get someone else's node — create fails permission-denied (read grant) or silently plants their "private" todoList under the sharer's admin-cascaded subtree (SEC: granting a new user `write` before first visit captures their container); (3) tier and `deleted` ignored.
- Fix: deterministic lookup — direct-`admin`-grant filter with a pinned onboarding invariant ("each user holds exactly one direct admin grant: their per-user node"), or a framework surface (`client.claims.nodeId` / slug===sub convention); guard the no-node case instead of `!`; check the create outcome.

**M2. ('lmz','dag') read/subscribe have no value source — bootstrap throws, auto-subscribe errors** *(ARCH; pairs with B2)*
- Location: `nebula-frontend.md:758`; `star.ts:300-365`; `resources.ts:248-255`; `subscriptions.ts` (throws on null); `coding-your-ui.md:276`
- Check-skips don't produce a value: the DAG has no Snapshots row, `Star.read` never receives resourceType on the wire, so `read('lmz','dag')` returns null (bootstrap's `!.value` throws at module load) and `subscribe` throws "cannot subscribe before create".
- Fix: special-case ('lmz','dag') in BOTH read and subscribe paths to synthesize the Snapshot from `dagTree.getState()` + synthesized meta; resolve the missing-resourceType dispatch (wire param or reserved-rid rule); state it in api-reference § DagTreeState.

**M3. Synthesized DAG snapshot `meta.typeName='DagTree'` breaks broadcast routing — tree views silently freeze after the initial push** *(ARCH + SEC converged)*
- Location: `nebula-frontend.md:1056` vs `star.ts:535-538`; `nebula-client.ts:1099-1130`
- `#broadcast` uses `snapshot.meta.typeName` as the rt; as pinned, mutations land at `store.resources.DagTree.dag` while all docs bind `store.resources.lmz.dag` — initial subscribe push is keyed correctly, so every subsequent update silently never arrives (stale permission/grey-out UX included).
- Fix: pin `meta.typeName: 'lmz'` (or hardcode rt='lmz' at the dag broadcast call site); record in the v3 checklist + api-reference § DagTreeState, superseding Phase -1 § 7.

**M4. Originator exclusion + no optimistic paint = mutating user's own tree never updates** *(ARCH + TEST converged)*
- Location: `nebula-frontend.md:1057`; `star.ts:492-494, :533`; `api-reference.md:386, :439`; `coding-your-ui.md:429-604`
- `#broadcast` excludes the originator (rationale: optimistic double-render), but `client.dagTree.*` returns only id/void with no local write-through and no handleTransactionResult — so after createNode/addEdge/setPermission, every OTHER client updates while the acting user's `store.resources.lmz.dag` stays stale. No test would catch it.
- Fix: pin a carve-out — dag broadcasts do NOT exclude the originator (no optimistic paint exists; deep-equals dedup absorbs the echo); record next to the BroadcastChannel pin; add test: client A mutates, A's own store reflects it.

**M5. `ready` failure semantics unspecified — unauthenticated visitor = permanent blank page** *(ARCH + TEST + PROD — 3 lenses converged; ARCH rated minor, TEST/PROD major)*
- Location: `api-reference.md:76, :84-98`; `nebula-studio.md:48-62`; `nebula-frontend.md:757`; substrate: `lumenize-client.ts:728-731, :824-838`
- Spec covers only resolution. On refresh 401 (logged-out visitor — the auto-detect cookie path hits this immediately), the failure falls into infinite reconnect backoff, `onLoginRequired` never fires on the first-connect path, `ready` hangs forever, top-level await blocks module evaluation → blank page with no error surface, in every Studio-generated app's first code path.
- Fix: pin in api-reference: `ready` rejects with a typed error on terminal auth failure (→ redirect to auth discovery); stays pending through transient network failure; mirror into nebula-studio.md; add v3 tests for 401-rejects and transient-then-resolves.

**M6. `client.claims.isAdmin` doesn't exist — admin gating example silently never renders** *(CONS)*
- Location: `coding-your-ui.md:184, :190`; `api-reference.md:490` vs `packages/nebula-auth/src/nebula-auth.ts:1358-1375`, `types.ts:41-72`
- The nebula-auth JWT nests admin at `payload.access.admin` (omitted when false); `client.claims` is the raw frozen payload, so `claims.isAdmin` is always `undefined` — the `v-if="client.claims.isAdmin"` pattern the Studio LLM will replicate never shows the button, for anyone.
- Fix: change both docs to `client.claims.access?.admin` (truthiness-safe), or add a claim-flattening v3 item if flat `isAdmin` is the desired contract.

**M7. Full permissions table rides the universal broadcast — contradicts "only structure is universal"** *(SEC)*
- Location: `resources.md:95` vs `api-reference.md:443-448 (§ DagTreeState)`; `dag-tree.ts getState()`
- Every connected client at any tier receives the tenant's complete ACL: all user subs (full enumeration) + who holds what tier on every node. This also makes B2's bypass cheap to target. The docs' own examples depend on permissions visibility, so the choice must be explicit either way.
- Fix: pin one direction — (a) full-ACL-universal by design, delete the contradicting sentence; or (b) filtered view (structure + own grants universal; per-node ACL on demand for admins) with examples adjusted.

**M8. `lmz`/`dag` namespace reservation unenforced server-side — rid collision cross-wires subscriptions** *(SEC + ARCH converged)*
- Location: `coding-your-ui.md:475`; `nebula-frontend.md:758` (no enforcement item); `subscriptions.ts:44-54` (no rt column, INSERT OR REPLACE); `star.ts:530-531`; `create-nebula-client.ts:295-309`
- Nothing rejects an ontology type named 'lmz' or an app resource with rid 'dag'. Subscribers PK is (resourceId, clientId): an app 'dag' resource and the synthetic tree become the SAME row — subscribe clobbers, unsubscribe kills both, each broadcasts to the other's (permission-unchecked) subscribers. Also nothing excludes rt 'lmz' from the synced-state middleware (stray store write → doomed transaction).
- Fix: v3 items — ontology compile rejects type 'lmz'; transaction path rejects creates on reserved rids (or key Subscribers by (rt, rid)); exclude `rt==='lmz'` from the middleware.

**M9. Per-call handler shadows per-type handlers for every resource in the batch — doc's own examples collide** *(TEST + PROD converged)*
- Location: `coding-your-ui.md:334, :360-377` vs `:295`; `api-reference.md:313, :331-334`; `nebula-frontend.md` 5.3.8 L944
- Forms' saveTodo per-call handler (returns undefined for non-task rids) suppresses the per-type todoList set-union/openCount handler that § Atomic append mandates 40 lines earlier — a concurrent list edit during a form save silently falls to framework use-server (openCount drift; also disables @longform per-type merges). The undefined-fall-through chain (per-call → per-type → default vs per-call → default) is unpinned and the 5.3.8 probe is single-type so it can't distinguish.
- Fix: pin overlay semantics (undefined falls through to per-type, then framework default) in api-reference § Precedence; align L334/L369 wording; add a mixed-type-batch conflict probe.

**M10. Map/Set collection mutations bypass sync — silent local-only divergence** *(ARCH)*
- Location: `resources.md:8`; `coding-your-ui.md:30/41`; `spike/.../create-nebula-client.ts:260-283`; `nebula-frontend.md:759`
- Write capture is a Proxy `set` trap; `Map.set`/`Set.add`/`delete` are method calls that never hit it — local Vue store updates, no transaction submits, divergence until the next fanout clobbers. Docs advertise full structured-clone types + invisible auto-sync, so the Studio LLM will generate `value.tags.add('foo')`. None of the 32 carry-forward tests covers a Map/Set-valued resource through the factory (read-side double-proxy also untested, and the DAG state is Maps/Sets).
- Fix: pin the contract in api-reference (auto-sync = property assignments; collection mutations need explicit transaction or container-replacement assignment); add a v3 Map/Set-valued-resource test (reads via store proxy; method-writes captured or debug-warned).

**M11. First-run read-then-create: race outcome unclassified, zero test obligations** *(TEST; follows from M1's pattern)*
- Location: `coding-your-ui.md:268-287`; `resources.ts:377-379`; `nebula-frontend.md` 5.3.8 (no probe); `nebula-studio.md:83`
- The doc's "second tab may lose the create — safe to ignore" is false as specced: the loser hits a plain throw → `infrastructure-error` (v3) / timeout (today), indistinguishable from an outage, triggering rollback and the recommended error toast. "Already exists" has no home in the outcome taxonomy; the whole bootstrap flow has no tests despite touching three v3 surfaces.
- Fix: pin the classification of create-on-existing (typed resolution, or re-read-on-failure guidance); add probes: full first-visit flow renders; two-client race → winner commits, loser matches the pinned outcome.

**M12. Tombstone contract: stale v3 checklist item specs the forbidden null→deleted model; client half untested** *(TEST)*
- Location: `nebula-frontend.md:674` vs `api-reference.md:519`, `coding-your-ui.md:137`; 5.3.8 (no tombstone probe)
- L674 still says "preserve meta.deleted: true when null comes in" — the pinned contract says deletions NEVER arrive as null (tombstones are real Snapshots). No test asserts the factory writes tombstones through or that the pattern-3 status computed yields 'deleted'.
- Fix: rewrite L674 to the pinned contract; add a deletion-fanout probe (last value truthy + meta.deleted, computed returns 'deleted', subsequent read returns the tombstone).

**M13. resources.md:378 still teaches the old per-client queue — "other resources queue behind the parked one"** *(CONS)*
- Location: `resources.md:378` vs `debounce-serial-queue.md:18`, `coding-your-ui.md:88`, `api-reference.md:358`, `nebula-frontend.md:89`
- Last surviving instance of the per-client-queue model; under the pinned per-(rt,rid) granularity, other resources flow independently.
- Fix: rewrite to "same-resource writes buffer behind the parked transaction; other resources flow through their own queues."

**M14. ('lmz','dag') is the only substantive v3 server item with zero test bullets** *(TEST; companion to M2/M3/B2)*
- Location: `nebula-frontend.md:758, :936-974`
- Untested: initial snapshot Map/Set wire fidelity (flagship rich-type consumer), the read path, the check-skip's capable-of-failing security probe, mutation→broadcast to a second subscriber, package exports.
- Fix: add a Tests sub-list covering all five + a dag probe in 5.3.8's framework-agnostic list.

**M15. api-reference:457 "writes to these paths are dropped" contradicts the doc's core write mechanism** *(ARCH + PROD + CONS — 3 lenses; CONS rated major, others minor)*
- Location: `api-reference.md:457` vs `:470, :75`
- Blanket sentence is false for the primary write surface (`resources.*.value.*` → synced middleware) and for `meta.*` (passes through, warned). An LLM reading the contract section could conclude v-model on resources is illegal.
- Fix: per-prefix truth — value.* = the supported sync path; meta.* = passes through, server-owned, debug-warned; lmz.* = framework-written only.

---

## MINORS (14)

**m1. § resources.read "Returns null… (deleted or never created)" — stale tombstone model inside the contract doc** *(SEC + PROD + CONS — 3 lenses; CONS rated major)*
- Location: `api-reference.md:193` vs `:519`; `resources.ts:205-215, :248`
- Fix: "Returns null only if never created. Soft-deleted resources return a real Snapshot with meta.deleted: true — see § Snapshot."

**m2. Connection-state replay: four artifacts, two stories; api-reference order caveat is vestigial** *(ARCH + PROD + CONS — 3 lenses)*
- Location: `api-reference.md:55` vs `nebula-frontend.md:756, :576, :554`
- Replay is pinned twice and strictly more forgiving (and the v3 config-shape factory makes "late registration" unviolatable). Fix: ship replay, record the 5.3.7 reversal, soften api-reference to "captures current state at creation."

**m3. debounce-serial-queue.md still pins/tests `v-model.eager`, which v3 retired** *(TEST + PROD + CONS — 3 lenses)*
- Location: `debounce-serial-queue.md:16, :42, :82` vs `nebula-frontend.md:579`
- The v3-gating D0 spike would validate a dead surface; the replacement (`@debounce(0)`/quietMs:0 + shortest-active-timer-wins) has no D0 assertion. Fix: replace the pin + probe; restate D2's blur-flush in the directive-free mechanism.

**m4. Stale checklist: "Expose client.claims on LumenizeClient" already fully shipped** *(ARCH + PROD + CONS — 3 lenses)*
- Location: `nebula-frontend.md:760-781` vs `lumenize-client.ts:384, :432, :483, :689, :930` (+ tests + mdx doc)
- Fix: check it off (residual: optional calls.mdx sweep), so v3 scope/estimate stops double-counting.

**m5. Package shape contradiction: `@lumenize/nebula-client` dep vs "moves into nebula-frontend"** *(ARCH + CONS — 2 lenses)*
- Location: `nebula-frontend.md:594, :666, :922` vs `:28, :43` (layout src/ omits nebula-client.ts; package doesn't exist)
- Fix: pin one package (`@lumenize/nebula-frontend` containing NebulaClient, matching the docs) and fix all three references incl. L666.

**m6. "Direct Workers-RPC call" — impossible mechanism + forbidden vocabulary in the contract doc** *(ARCH + PROD — 2 lenses)*
- Location: `api-reference.md:386`
- Fix: "request/response mesh call, Promise-correlated client-side; failures reject" + one-sentence rationale for reject-vs-always-resolve.

**m7. `saveTodo` missing the `if (!list) return` guard its sibling `addTodo` has** *(PROD + CONS — 2 lenses)*
- Location: `coding-your-ui.md:353-365`
- Status change before todoList snapshot arrives → TypeError on `list.openCount`. Fix: mirror the guard.

**m8. `onShouldRefreshUI` type parses wrong + `OntologyStaleInfo` undefined in the doc** *(ARCH + PROD + CONS — 3 lenses)*
- Location: `api-reference.md:67`
- Fix: `((info: OntologyStaleInfo) => void) | null`; add the OntologyStaleInfo shape definition (lives only in the task file today).

**m9. Serial queue × multi-resource batches unspecified at the D0/D1 gate** *(ARCH)*
- Location: `debounce-serial-queue.md:11-20` vs `coding-your-ui.md:293-317, :330-394`
- Fix: add a multi-resource clause (batch occupies all touched (rt,rid) queues; buffered writes chain off batch eTag; explicit calls flush pending debounced writes) + a D0 race probe.

**m10. Per-op permission tiers for resource ops stated nowhere (move's two-node rule underivable)** *(SEC)*
- Location: `resources.md:96`; `api-reference.md:210-226`; contract in `resources.ts:403-414`
- Fix: one-line tier table — create=write@nodeId; put/delete=write@attached; move=write@both; read/subscribe=read.

**m11. Revocation doesn't cut live subscriptions — docs imply it does** *(SEC)*
- Location: `resources.md:94-97` ("gated at every read"); `star.ts` #broadcast comment
- Fix: one sentence — read permission checked at subscribe; revocation takes effect on disconnect/unsubscribe/deploy.

**m12. Claims typing: `Readonly<JwtPayload> | null` makes every blessed example a strict-TS error** *(PROD)*
- Location: `api-reference.md:486-494`; `lumenize-client.ts:483`
- Fix: pin (a) NebulaClient narrows to non-null (justified by `ready`), or (b) one documented `!` convention. (a) preferred.

**m13. Test-gap pair: store.ui/app pre-seed unasserted; auto-subscribe error path untested client-side** *(TEST ×2, merged)*
- Location: `nebula-frontend.md:671, :759`; 5.3.8 L956-974; spike `.catch` swallow
- Fix: factory-basics assertions (ui/app exist reactive-empty, no deep vivification, never hit middleware); Vue probe for bad-rid/no-permission subscribe (no unhandled rejection, path stays undefined, debug-sink warning, refcount cleanup).

**m14. Doc/task hygiene batch (each one verified, one-line fixes):**
- `nebula-studio.md:44` vs `:83` — "LLM never touches bootstrap files" vs "nebula.ts is the LLM extension point" *(PROD)*
- `nebula-frontend.md:660` — "~2 days" v3 estimate materially stale (closer to 1-2 weeks; auto-detect hides a nebula-auth global-refresh endpoint); split v3a/v3b/v3c *(PROD)*
- `nebula-frontend.md:215` — phantom `TxnEntries` in § Surface; should be `Record<string, OperationDescriptor>` *(CONS)*
- `nebula-frontend.md:101` — ontology-stale wrongly listed as rollback (v3 pin: no rollback) *(CONS)*
- `nebula-frontend.md:555` — flash-on-use-server-only row superseded (all four rollback outcomes flash) *(CONS)*
- `resources.md:35` — awaited subscribe teaches the pre-v3 Promise idiom; drop the `await` *(CONS)*
- `api-reference.md:248-255` — multi-resource example uses pre-unification ontology (`done: false`, 'list-main', no openCount) *(CONS)*
- `coding-your-ui.md:276` — dag read missing `as DagTreeState` cast → `unknown` type error in the flagship bootstrap *(PROD)*
- Status-tag accuracy: § read/transaction tags omit appVersion rename + OperationDescriptor reshape (new-in-v3); § Reserved paths/lmz.connection tagged implemented-in-spike but seeds are v3; Summary-table rows under-report split tags, client.claims has a non-legend tag + no row, § Middleware untagged *(CONS ×3)*

---

## NITS (4)

**n1.** Idempotent no-ops (existing edge, already-deleted node, absent grant) return success BEFORE the permission check, contradicting "every method requires permission" — add one doc clause. `api-reference.md:388-432` vs `dag-tree.ts:202-360` *(SEC)*
**n2.** `coding-your-ui.md:33` says bootstrap exports `{ client, store }` — should be `{ client, store, ready }` *(CONS)*
**n3.** `coding-your-ui.md:37` lists bare `transaction`/`subscribe` as client methods — namespace them `resources.*` *(CONS)*
**n4.** Dead anchor: `nebula-frontend.md:732` links `#auto-subscribe` → now `#subscription-lifecycle` *(CONS)*

---

## Biggest risks

The ('lmz','dag') Option-0 pull-in is dangerously under-specified end-to-end — keying (B2), value sourcing (M2), broadcast routing (M3), originator exclusion (M4), and namespace enforcement (M8) each independently break or compromise the flagship demo path, and two are security holes (B2 bypass, B1 addEdge escalation) shipped in the substrate, not just the spec. Second cluster: the brand-new first-run bootstrap + `ready` contract (M1, M5, M11) crashes or blanks the app for exactly the first users a demo meets — admins, logged-out visitors, and racing tabs. Third: the published "retry-safe"/"preserves both edits" contracts are currently false in code (B3 create replay, B4 textMerge base), so v3 cannot make the docs true without unlisted server fixes.
